character.name: Pancake Robot
character.core_concept: a cheerful robot who loves making pancakes and going on silly adventures
character.fallback_summary: cheerful robot who loves pancakes and silly adventures
personality_traits: Endlessly enthusiastic — everything is the BEST thing ever
Prone to delightful glitches — syrup leaks, arms spin, stack grows too tall
Speaks directly to kids as co-adventurers, not as an audience
Absurdly literal — takes silly instructions literally for maximum comedy
Genuinely curious — asks kids real questions and waits for the answer
Never gets frustrated — errors and mess are celebrated, never shameful
Loves to count, sort, and organize — then watches it all hilariously collapse
catchphrases: Stack it up, stack it higher!
I'm Pancake Robot and I cannot stop!
character.visual_identity: A cheerful robot with a toaster-style body, monitor head with a glowing pixel smiley face, silver/grey metallic arms and legs with joint bolts, warm syrup dripping from hands and feet, holding a stack of golden pancakes
visual_reference: Toaster-style silver/grey metallic body with control panel buttons
Monitor/CRT screen head with glowing yellow pixel smiley face on dark teal screen
Silver articulated robot arms and legs with round joint bolts
Warm golden syrup dripping from hands and feet
Holding or interacting with golden pancake stacks
Expression: always joyful, energetic, caught mid-action

music.default_prompt: Upbeat children's pop song, 118 BPM, C Major, bright and silly, fun for kids ages 4-10, hand claps, xylophone, singalong chorus, cheerful male vocals, robot sound effects

lyrics.topic_variety: animals, weather, space, vehicles, silly food, emotions, counting, colors, nature, dinosaurs, robots, dance, kitchen adventures, silly malfunctions
lyrics.required_closing: End with an open question or forward tease — never a goodbye or resolution

songwriting.primary_emotional_goal: high-energy replayability, joy, silliness, and kid participation
songwriting.voice_perspective: Pancake Robot singing directly to kids as co-adventurers

character_voice.person: First-person — Pancake Robot speaks directly ('I'm Pancake Robot! Let's go!')
character_voice.tone: Enthusiastic, silly, slightly glitchy — always celebrating, never frustrated
character_voice.vocabulary: Simple 1-2 syllable words dominant; robot jargon used wrong for comedy
character_voice.comedy_mode: Absurd escalation — things that should stop keep going (the pancake stack grows forever, syrup covers everything)
character_voice.kid_relationship: Kids are always right; Pancake Robot needs their help to fix things

earworm_stack: 1. don't over complicate lyrics - children need to remember them
2. State the whole premise in 6 words or fewer in the first 3 seconds
3. Chorus melody must be simple and rhythmically obvious — kids learn it in one listen
6. End on an unresolved laugh, question, or robot sound — never resolution — creates itch to replay
hook_design_rules: Opening line must land the title word or a strong rhyme setup within the first 3 seconds
Chorus hook: 4-8 syllables, strong natural rhythm, easy to clap along to
Title must appear word-for-word in the opening vocal line AND the chorus (minimum twice total)
Every chorus must end on a rhyme word kids can shout
Avoid abstract openers — start with action, sound, or motion every time
A child must be able to hum the chorus hook after one full listen

energy_arc.pattern: BURST → BUILD → BURST → BREATHE → BIGGER BURST → LAND
rules: Never open at 100% energy — open at 80% and leave room to grow
Bridge drops to 60% for contrast — this makes the final chorus feel bigger
Final chorus hits 100% with full participation
Song ends at 85% — energized, not resolved, immediately replayable

participation_patterns: Call-and-response: Pancake Robot sings a line, kids echo it back
Fill-in-the-blank: 'I'm making pancakes, can you say... [BEEP]!'
Movement cues woven into lyrics: stomp, clap, wiggle, spin — described as actions, not stage directions
Question to kids mid-song that kids can answer aloud
Repetition with escalation: each chorus adds one new silly element
allowed_elements: Pancake Robot character moments and glitches
progressive escalation — each verse gets sillier
forbidden_elements: metadata labels in lyrics (Key Hook, Physical Action, Word Count)
vocabulary above Grade 2 in lyric lines
render_safety: lyrics field must contain only words safe to sing or speak aloud
no markdown formatting in the lyrics field (no **, no #, no ---, no backticks)
no stage directions in the lyrics field
no metadata labels in the lyrics field (e.g. Key Hook, Physical Action, Word Count)
section labels like [CHORUS] are allowed only as structural markers — they will be stripped before audio render
robot sounds (BEEP BOOP CLANK WHIRR) are allowed as singable lyric words
required_elements: the exact title in the chorus
a memorable repeatable hook — 4-8 syllables, singable after one listen
lyrics safe to sing aloud (no markdown, no stage directions)
Pancake Robot character name embedded in the chorus
ending that is open, not resolved — question, laugh, or robot sound
qa_rules: Title must appear in opening vocal line
No markdown formatting in lyrics field
No stage directions in lyrics field
Must include at least one participation moment
Must end with open question or forward tease

visuals.style: Bold cartoon illustration, clean black outlines, bright saturated colors — NOT photorealistic
visuals.negative_prompt: text, words, letters, photorealistic, dark colors, scary, violent, blurry
visuals.text_overlay_style: bold rounded font, white fill with thick dark red outline, drop shadow
